preamble: This phonetic transformation involves the fronting of the initial element of the back rounded diphthong /ou/ and a subsequent adjustment in the second element, leading to the formation of the fronted diphthong /oi/. This shift typically results in a more fronted and often less rounded articulation of the diphthong.
phoneticEffects: Diphthongal fronting, Vowel quality shift, Diphthongal centralization/unrounding

examples.note: A comparison of the same word in Norse illustrating the diphthongal shift from /ou/ to /oi/.

sources: Flom, G. T. (1915). Scandinavian Influence on Southern Lowland Scotch: A Contribution to the Study of the Linguistic Relations of English and Scandinavian. Columbia University Press.
